goal: Open Phase 2 by extending `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` so `PluginManifest` parses `[capabilities]` (provides / languages / activation rules) and `[resources]` per master-plan §14.1, and so `PluginRuntime` exposes the FULL DISCOVERED → REGISTERED → LOADING → ACTIVE → IDLE → STOPPED → ERROR state machine with monitored, `tracing`-logged transitions and a captured error message on the ERROR state. Two new acceptance tests at the module root: `plugin_manager::test_manifest_parser` (P2-W6-F01) and `plugin_manager::test_lifecycle_state_machine` (P2-W6-F02). No other crate, no other module, no fixture changes.
plan_summary: Three changes in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`. (1) Manifest schema: add `pub capabilities: CapabilitiesSection` (default-on, fields: `provides: Vec<String>`, `languages: Vec<String>`, `activation: ActivationSection { on_language, on_tool, eager }`) and `pub resources: Option<ResourcesSection> { memory_mb, startup_time_ms, typical_query_ms }` per master-plan §14.1. Add a public `validate(&self) -> Result<(), PluginError>` method that rejects empty `plugin.name`, empty `plugin.version`, empty `transport.kind`, empty `transport.command`, and a `capabilities.activation.on_language` containing a language not in `capabilities.languages`. Add helper `pub fn activates_for_language(&self, lang: &str) -> bool` and `pub fn activates_for_tool(&self, tool: &str) -> bool`. (2) Lifecycle state machine: add `pub error_message: Option<String>` to `PluginRuntime`; add transition methods `register(&mut self) -> Result<(), TransitionError>` (Discovered → Registered), `mark_loading(&mut self) -> Result<(), TransitionError>` (Registered/Idle → Loading), `mark_active(&mut self) -> Result<(), TransitionError>` (Loading → Active, sets `last_call = Instant::now()`), `stop(&mut self) -> Result<(), TransitionError>` (any non-Error → Stopped), `mark_error(&mut self, msg: impl Into<String>)` (any → Error, captures message). Each transition emits one `tracing::info!(plugin = %self.manifest.plugin.name, from = %old, to = %new, "plugin lifecycle transition")` line, except `mark_error` which uses `tracing::warn!(...)`. Reject illegal transitions with a new `TransitionError { from: PluginState, to: PluginState }` variant under `PluginError`. (3) Frozen-selector acceptance tests at the module root (NOT inside `mod tests { }`, per DEC-0007 precedent set by `test_hot_cold_lifecycle`): `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_manifest_parser()` parses a fixture `plugin.toml` covering ALL master-plan §14.1 sections (plugin/capabilities/transport/resources/lifecycle), asserts every field, calls `validate()` on a happy and a sad case (empty name → error), and asserts `activates_for_language("rust") == true` / `activates_for_tool("foo") == false`; `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_lifecycle_state_machine()` walks one `PluginRuntime` through Discovered → Registered → Loading → Active → Idle → Loading → Active → Stopped, then a separate runtime through Registered → Error, asserting every state, every `error_message`, and that a `tracing` test-subscriber recorded ≥6 transition events (use `tracing-subscriber`'s `with_test_writer` already present in the dev-deps tree, or fall back to a plain counter via the existing `[dev-dependencies]` if the writer isn't available). Both new tests use the existing `mock_mcp_plugin_path()` helper for any health-check segments — no new fixtures.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `CapabilitiesSection`, `ActivationSection`, `ResourcesSection` `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]` structs. `CapabilitiesSection` MUST `#[serde(default)]` so existing minimal manifests in the integration test (`tests/plugin_manager.rs`) keep parsing — this is a backward-compat invariant. `ActivationSection` fields: `pub on_language: Vec<String>`, `pub on_tool: Vec<String>`, `pub eager: bool` — all `#[serde(default)]`. `ResourcesSection` fields: `pub memory_mb: Option<u64>`, `pub startup_time_ms: Option<u64>`, `pub typical_query_ms: Option<u64>`. Wire into `PluginManifest`: `#[serde(default)] pub capabilities: CapabilitiesSection`, `#[serde(default)] pub resources: Option<ResourcesSection>`.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), PluginError>` on `PluginManifest`. Validation rules (master-plan §14.1 + spec common-sense): (a) `plugin.name` non-empty, no whitespace; (b) `plugin.version` non-empty (no semver-strictness in this WO); (c) `transport.kind` non-empty; (d) `transport.command` non-empty; (e) every `capabilities.activation.on_language` entry ALSO appears in `capabilities.languages` when `capabilities.languages` is non-empty (otherwise activation rules trivially permitted). New `PluginError::InvalidManifest { field: &'static str, reason: String }` variant. `PluginManifest::from_path` calls `.validate()` BEFORE returning so callers always get a well-formed manifest.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `pub fn activates_for_language(&self, language: &str) -> bool` and `pub fn activates_for_tool(&self, tool: &str) -> bool` on `PluginManifest`. Semantics: empty `on_language` ⇒ activates for every language (the master-plan default for plugins that don't filter); non-empty ⇒ membership test. Same rule for `on_tool`. Both functions are zero-allocation (use `.iter().any(|s| s == language)`).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — extend `PluginRuntime`: add `pub error_message: Option<String>` (default `None`), keep all existing fields. The `tick`/`mark_call`/`with_idle_timeout` surface stays unchanged so the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` test continues to pass without edits.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add transition methods on `PluginRuntime` returning `Result<(), TransitionError>`: `pub fn register(&mut self)` (Discovered → Registered, also resets `error_message = None`), `pub fn mark_loading(&mut self)` (Registered or Idle → Loading), `pub fn mark_active(&mut self)` (Loading → Active, sets `last_call = Instant::now()`), `pub fn stop(&mut self)` (any non-Error, non-Stopped → Stopped), `pub fn mark_error(&mut self, msg: impl Into<String>)` (any → Error, captures `error_message`, never returns an error). Each method emits exactly one `tracing::info!(target: "ucil.plugin.lifecycle", plugin = %self.manifest.plugin.name, from = %old_state, to = %new_state, "plugin lifecycle transition")` line, except `mark_error` which uses `tracing::warn!(target: "ucil.plugin.lifecycle", plugin = %self.manifest.plugin.name, error = %msg, "plugin entered ERROR state")`. The span/target naming follows master-plan §15.2 (`ucil.<layer>.<op>`).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add `PluginError::InvalidManifest { field, reason }` variant AND a peer `pub struct TransitionError { pub from: PluginState, pub to: PluginState }` with `Display`/`std::error::Error` impls (or fold into `PluginError::IllegalTransition { from, to }` — implementer's choice). Either way, illegal transitions (e.g., `register()` from `Active`) MUST return an error; they MUST NOT panic and MUST NOT silently accept the no-op.
crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — extend `impl PluginManifest::from_path` to call `manifest.validate()?` before returning. This is the only callsite-side change.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — module-root `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_manifest_parser()` (NOT in `mod tests { }`). Reads a `tempfile::tempdir()`-hosted `plugin.toml` containing every §14.1 section (plugin / capabilities / transport / resources / lifecycle). Asserts: parsed `plugin.name == "semgrep-fixture"`, `capabilities.provides` matches expected slice, `capabilities.languages` matches, `capabilities.activation.on_language` matches, `capabilities.activation.on_tool` matches, `capabilities.activation.eager == false`, `resources.as_ref().unwrap().memory_mb == Some(200)`, `transport.kind == "stdio"`, `lifecycle.as_ref().unwrap().hot_cold == true`. Then a sad-path block writes a manifest with empty `plugin.name` and asserts `from_path` returns `PluginError::InvalidManifest { field: "plugin.name", .. }`. Final block: build a `PluginManifest` value via struct literal with `capabilities.activation.on_language = vec!["rust".into()]` and `capabilities.languages = vec!["rust".into(), "typescript".into()]`, assert `activates_for_language("rust") == true`, `activates_for_language("go") == false`, `activates_for_tool("foo") == false` when `on_tool` is empty? — NO, empty on_tool means "activates for every tool" so the assertion is `activates_for_tool("anything") == true`. Re-check the doc comment to keep semantics consistent.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — module-root `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_lifecycle_state_machine()` (NOT in `mod tests { }`). Build a `PluginManifest` in-memory (no real `plugin.toml`, no real subprocess — this test exercises the state machine only). Construct a `PluginRuntime` via `PluginRuntime::new(...)` — initial state is `Registered` per current API. To exercise the full machine, manually flip `runtime.state = PluginState::Discovered` first (or call a new `PluginRuntime::discovered(manifest)` constructor — implementer's call as long as the test path covers `Discovered → Registered`). Drive the machine: `register()` → `Registered`, `mark_loading()` → `Loading`, `mark_active()` → `Active`, force-tick `Active → Idle` by setting `idle_timeout = Duration::from_millis(1)` and back-dating `last_call` then `runtime.tick(Instant::now())`, `mark_loading()` → `Loading`, `mark_active()` → `Active`, `stop()` → `Stopped`. Assert each state. Assert that calling `register()` from `Active` returns an `IllegalTransition`/`TransitionError` error WITHOUT changing state. Build a SECOND `PluginRuntime`; call `mark_error("boom")` from `Registered`; assert `state == Error` and `error_message.as_deref() == Some("boom")`. The test does NOT spawn a subprocess. The test does NOT install a global tracing subscriber (avoid global-state contamination) — it asserts state transitions directly.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — extend the rustdoc on `PluginManifest`, `PluginState`, and `PluginRuntime` to mention the new sections / transitions. Keep additions SHORT; the crate is under `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` and prior phase-1 WOs have been rejected for over-eager rustdoc links. Use plain backticks or fully qualified paths only.

crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — NO new deps required (toml + tracing + thiserror + tokio + serde + tempfile already in [dependencies] / [dev-dependencies]). If the executor finds a missing one (e.g. `tracing-subscriber` for the lifecycle test) the executor may add it under `[dev-dependencies]` ONLY, with a one-line commit message rationale.

Commit ladder — approximately 5 commits, each ≤50 LOC: (1) `feat(daemon): add CapabilitiesSection + ResourcesSection to plugin manifest schema` (~50 LOC structs + serde wiring); (2) `feat(daemon): add PluginManifest::validate + activates_for_language/tool helpers` (~40 LOC validation + helpers + InvalidManifest error variant); (3) `feat(daemon): add full plugin lifecycle transition methods + tracing logs` (~60 LOC: register/mark_loading/mark_active/stop/mark_error + TransitionError + log emission); (4) `test(daemon): add plugin_manager::test_manifest_parser frozen-selector acceptance test` (~70 LOC; over-budget per DEC-0005 module-root test commit precedent); (5) `test(daemon): add plugin_manager::test_lifecycle_state_machine acceptance test` (~80 LOC; same DEC-0005 precedent). Soft target ≤50 LOC/commit; the test commits MAY exceed.
scope_out: NO changes to ANY other crate: `ucil-core`, `ucil-treesitter`, `ucil-cli`, `ucil-lsp-diagnostics`, `ucil-embeddings`, `ucil-agents`. Plugin CLI subcommands (P2-W6-F07) live in ucil-cli and are deferred to a sibling WO.
NO new module, NO renamed module — every change lands inside the existing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs`, `server.rs`, `executor.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `storage.rs`, `startup.rs`, `priority_queue.rs`, `text_search.rs`, `understand_code.rs`, `watcher.rs`, `test_support.rs`, or `lib.rs`. The new `Capabilities*` / `Resources*` / `TransitionError` types are made `pub` at the module root and re-exported through whatever existing `pub use plugin_manager::*` glob `lib.rs` already exposes — if any new symbol is NOT covered by an existing glob, the executor MUST cite that gap in the ready-for-review note (and ONLY then add the explicit `pub use` line in `lib.rs`).

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manager.rs` or `tests/e2e_mcp_stdio.rs` or `tests/e2e_mcp_with_kg.rs` — frozen integration suites. The new acceptance tests are MODULE-ROOT in `src/plugin_manager.rs` (per DEC-0007) so they appear under `plugin_manager::test_*` selectors.
NO hot-reload, NO circuit-breakers, NO `ucil plugin` CLI, NO ast-grep / Probe manifests, NO plugin-lifecycle integration suite. Those are P2-W6-F03..F08 and live in sibling work-orders.
NO HOT/COLD lifecycle changes — the existing `tick` / `mark_call` / `with_idle_timeout` surface is preserved untouched so the frozen `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` (P1-W3-F06) keeps passing.
NO changes to `Cargo.toml` `[workspace.dependencies]` table at the repo root. If a new dev-dep is needed, it lands under `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies]` only.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, `it.skip`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.
NO `git commit --no-verify` / `--amend` after push / force-push — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

NO global tracing subscriber installation in the new tests — avoid cross-test contamination; the lifecycle test asserts state transitions directly via the public `state` / `error_message` fields. Subscriber-based assertion is left for a later integration test (P2-W6-F08).
acceptance: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_manifest_parser` passes (frozen selector for P2-W6-F01).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_lifecycle_state_machine` passes (frozen selector for P2-W6-F02).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle` still passes (P1-W3-F06 regression guard).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib` passes (no in-process regression in any other module).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manager` passes (existing integration test still green — backward-compat for older minimal manifests is preserved by `#[serde(default)]` on the new sections).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio` passes (Phase-1 stub-path regression guard).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_with_kg` passes (Phase-1 KG-bootstrap regression guard).
`cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` passes — no regression in any other crate.
`cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` emits no `^error` and no `^warning: unresolved` lines (rustdoc-link landmine guard from prior phase-1 rejections).

Mutation check (verifier runs): stash the body of `PluginRuntime::register` so it becomes a no-op → `test_lifecycle_state_machine` fails (the `assert_eq!(state, Registered)` after `register()` trips); restore → green. Proves the new transition method is the sole reason the assertion holds.

Mutation check (verifier runs): stash the body of `PluginManifest::validate` so it returns `Ok(())` unconditionally → `test_manifest_parser` fails (the empty-name sad-path assertion trips); restore → green.

dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W3-F05: passes=true (last_verified_ts 2026-04-16). WO-0009 landed `PluginManifest::from_path`, `PluginManager::discover`, `PluginManager::spawn`, `PluginManager::health_check` at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` — extension points consumed unchanged by this WO.

lessons_applied: DEC-0007 frozen-selector placement: both new acceptance tests land at the MODULE ROOT of `src/plugin_manager.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { }`) so nextest reports them under `plugin_manager::test_manifest_parser` / `plugin_manager::test_lifecycle_state_machine` — exactly the selectors frozen in feature-list.json. Same pattern as the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` (lines 800+).

Backward-compat for serde defaults: every new manifest section is `#[serde(default)]` so the existing minimal manifests in `tests/plugin_manager.rs` (which only set `[plugin]` and `[transport]`) still parse without edits. Failing this means the integration suite breaks — guarded by an explicit acceptance criterion that runs the existing test.

Rustdoc link discipline: prior phase-1 rejections (WO-0009 / WO-0024 / WO-0025 / WO-0027 / WO-0039) all stemmed from `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` triggering on a shorthand link. This WO keeps rustdoc additions SHORT and uses plain backticks or fully qualified paths only. Acceptance criterion runs `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps`.

Cargo-test summary-line regex drift (WO-0038/0039 lesson): acceptance_criteria uses `grep -Eq` with alternation that matches BOTH cargo-test (`test result: ok. N passed; 0 failed`) AND cargo-nextest (`N tests run: M passed`) summary-line shapes.
Mutation-check pre-bake: acceptance includes two explicit verifier-runs-mutation entries (one per feature) so the verifier doesn't have to invent the mutation patch. Stash bodies named explicitly.

No-mocks-of-critical-deps invariant from CLAUDE.md: this WO does NOT spawn a real plugin subprocess in either acceptance test; the lifecycle test drives the in-memory state machine ONLY (the existing `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` is the integration-tier coverage that DOES spawn a real subprocess and stays unchanged).
DEC-0005 module-root test commit budget: commits 4 and 5 (the two test-introduction commits) MAY exceed the ~50 LOC soft target. Documented in scope_in commit ladder.

Single-file-edit blast radius: the entire WO touches one source file (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`) plus optionally `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies]`. Forbidden-paths list is correspondingly tight.
Logging discipline: `tracing::info!`/`warn!` only — NO `println!` / `eprintln!` (rust-style.md forbids those in library code). Target `ucil.plugin.lifecycle` aligns with master-plan §15.2.

Phase-1 regression guards: acceptance_criteria runs `e2e_mcp_stdio` AND `e2e_mcp_with_kg` AND the existing `tests/plugin_manager.rs` suite — three explicit regression sub-checks so this WO can not silently break a Phase-1 acceptance test.
